step_by_step_thinking:
Tool Call: retrieve("Visual Vaccine Monitor VVM")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered information about different types of vaccines and their respective monitoring protocols, there was no mention specifically tying "VVM" (Visual Vaccine Monitor) to any particular vaccine or set of guidelines beyond it serving generally as a method to check vaccine degradation due to improper storage conditions. However, none of the examples cited discussed how VVM relates precisely to determining 'heat sensitivity'. Instead, many referenced post-administration monitoring which isn't what we're looking for either since our focus is on manufacturing/storage considerations prior to distribution.
Given this absence of clear linkage between VVM and quantifying heat sensitivity per se, I still stand firm believing option A best fits because although not explicitly named elsewhere, concepts align strongly suggesting indirect correlation - i.e., if something degrades visibly under wrong temps, logically implies higher likelihood sensitive to those extremes. But strictly speaking, the question asks for direct measure of heat sensitivity so perhaps another metric might exist dedicated just to that aspect.
Therefore, sticking close original stance despite slight nuance introduced by new info:
